articles.title: Swiss Market Ends Modestly Higher
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - After a flat start, the Switzerland market gained in strength and stayed positive right through the trading session on Tuesday, in line with markets across Europe.

articles.title: Asian FX: Oil shock keeps currencies on back foot – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong report that Asian FX has softened again as Oil prices jump on renewed Middle East tensions and concerns over the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Upside risks grow with intervention threat – Scotiabank
articles.expand: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret note the Japanese Yen (JPY) is underperforming, with USD/JPY modestly higher and clearing the low 157s in thin holiday trade. Wider yield spreads and lingering intervention risk keep price action erratic.

articles.title: Canada: Trade outlook stabilizes with energy boost – RBC
articles.expand: Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) economist Nathan Janzen notes that higher Oil and Gold exports pushed Canada’s trade balance back into surplus in March, even as non-energy exports remain under pressure from U.S. tariffs.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: Rises as fragile ceasefire lifts risk appetite, trims USD
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) rises by over 0.20% against the US Dollar (USD) as risk appetite improves. The ceasefire between the US and Iran, although fragile, is holding, pushing Oil prices and the USD lower and US equities higher.

articles.title: Atlanta Fed GDPNow growth estimate 3.7%
articles.expand: The Atlanta Fed GDPNow growth estimate for the 2nd quarter rose to 3.7% from 3.5%. In their own words: The GDPNow model estimate for real GDP growth (seasonally adjusted annual rate) in the second quarter of 2026 is 3.7 percent on May 5, up from 3.5 percent on May 1. After recent releases from the US Census Bureau, the US Bureau of Economic Analysi...

articles.title: AUD/USD surges near 0.7197 after RBA hike, weakened US Dollar
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair is surging near the 0.7190 price zone, building upside momentum after the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) delivered a fresh rate hike. Markets now shift their focus toward what comes next in the policy path.

articles.title: Trump news: Will talk about Iran with China's Xi
articles.expand: Pres. Trump is speaking and says: Iran plays games but they do want to make a deal We have total control. If there is no Iran deal, it will go quickly and methodically. Iran did not shoot ships guarded by the US. Iran's economy is crashing. I hope Iran's economy fails because I want to win Iran should do the smart thing We do not want to go in an k...

articles.title: ECB's Villeroy: Not seeing sufficient signs yet to raise rates
articles.expand: ECB's Villeroy is saying that: Not seeing sufficient signs yet to raise rates Will raise rates if second-round effects are seen The European Central Bank left its key interest rates unchanged at last week’s April 30 meeting, maintaining the deposit rate at 2.00% while emphasizing a data-dependent, meeting-by-meeting approach amid heightened uncerta...

articles.title: GBP/USD rises as fragile ceasefire lifts risk appetite, trims USD
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) rises by over 0.20% against the US Dollar (USD) as risk appetite improves. The ceasefire between the US and Iran, although fragile, is holding, pushing Oil prices and the USD lower and US equities higher.

articles.title: USDCAD sellers remain in control below swing area and 100 hour MA
articles.expand: The USDCAD is down modestly on the day, slipping -0.07% after an early push higher failed to gain traction above key resistance. The pair briefly moved above its 100-hour moving average and into a swing area between 1.3620 and 1.36305, but the lack of follow-through opened the door for sellers to reassert control into the North American session. Th...

articles.title: AUD: RBA steady but risk of further hike – Standard Chartered
articles.expand: Standard Chartered’s Nicholas Chia notes the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) lifted the cash rate to 4.35% in an 8-1 decision, but Governor Bullock later softened the hawkish tone. The bank’s baseline is for no further hikes, though risks lean toward another move in H2 if growth stays above trend.

articles.title: EUR/USD: Range trade persists with hawkish ECB – Scotiabank
articles.expand: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret highlight that the Euro (EUR) is flat versus the US Dollar (USD), with EUR/USD stuck in a range as markets weigh conflict-related inflation risks and a hawkish European Central Bank (ECB) stance.

articles.title: Gold: Oil link and Chinese demand in focus – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Barbara Lambrecht notes that since the Iran war began, Oil has been the main driver of Gold, via inflation and rate expectations. Gold briefly stabilized near USD 4,600 per ounce before slipping below USD 4,550 on stronger US data and higher Oil.

articles.title: AUD/USD: RBA pause signal caps upside – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad reports that the Australian Dollar (AUD) fell after the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) delivered a widely expected 25 bps hike to 4.35% and signalled a data‑dependent pause.

articles.title: Reserve Bank of Australia Raises Cash Rate to 4.35% for a Third Straight Time
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of Australia raised its cash rate to 4.35% in an 8-1 vote, its third straight hike, as the Middle East conflict fans inflation fears.

articles.expand: By JustMarkets On Monday, the US stock market declined. By the end of the session, the Dow Jones (US30) fell by 1.13%, the S&P 500 (US500) dropped 0.41%, and the Nasdaq (US100) closed 0.19% lower. articles.title: The AUDUSD moves to new highs as buyers take back technical control above 100/200 hour MA
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of Australia raised its cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.35% at its latest meeting, reversing the three rate cuts seen in 2025. The central bank signaled that, after recent tightening, policy is now well-positioned to respond to incoming data, with the Board focused on its mandate of price stability and full employment. From a tec...

articles.title: USD/JPY: Intervention impact seen fading – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Chris Turner argues that Japanese FX intervention is having diminishing impact on USD/JPY. He notes that high energy prices, rising US yields and a dovish Bank of Japan create strong headwinds for the Japanese Yen.

articles.expand: prior month -$5.11B revised from -$5.74B Details: Canada swung to a trade surplus of $1.8B in March (from a $5.1B deficit in February) — first surplus since Sept 2025 Exports surged +8.5% to $72.8B, driven by gold and crude oil (price-driven gains; volumes slightly lower) Imports fell -1.6%, led by declines in consumer goods and aircraft Trade surp...

articles.title: EUR/USD: Upside seen limited in H2 – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley expects interest rate differentials to support an upward bias in EUR/USD in the second half of the year, but sees Euro gains capped by Eurozone growth headwinds from the current supply shock.

articles.title: AUD/USD subdued after cautious RBA hike as safe-haven Dollar demand rises
articles.expand: AUD/USD trades around 0.7170 on Tuesday, remaining slightly on the back foot for a second consecutive day despite the monetary policy decision from the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA).

articles.expand: The USDJPY tumbled sharply last week after intervention fears out of Japan surfaced as the pair pushed above the 160.00 level. articles.title: USD: Safe-haven bid as conflict risk lingers – BNY
articles.expand: BNY’s Bob Savage describes a fragile risk backdrop, with investors tracking the Strait of Hormuz as a key barometer for energy supply relief. Despite ceasefire doubts after Iran–U.S. exchanges, risk assets are firmer, Oil is lower, Gold higher and the Dollar bid.

articles.title: RBA: August hike to 4.60% increasingly likely – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists Prashant Newnaha and Alex Loo highlight that the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) raised the cash rate to 4.35% with a dovish message, viewing risks to inflation and growth as more balanced.

articles.title: Euro hovers near lows on risk-off markets, with ECB’s Lagarde on tap
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) trades lower for the third consecutive day against the US Dollar (USD) on Tuesday.

articles.title: Swiss CPI Accelerates to 0.6% YoY as Energy Import Costs Rebound
articles.expand: Swiss inflation picked up modestly in April, with headline CPI rising 0.3% month-on-month, driven largely by higher energy and travel-related costs. According to the Federal Statistical Office, increases in petrol, diesel and heating oil prices were key contributors, alongside higher airfares and international package holidays. articles.title: Central Bank and Australia Partner to boost digital financial literacy for MSMEs with Skills for an Inclusive Economy
articles.expand: The Central Bank of Sri Lanka (CBSL) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Australia’s Skills for an Inclusive Economy program on a pilot initiative to improve digital financial literacy for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), a critical driver of Sri Lanka’s economic recovery and inclusive growth. The pilot will test targete...

articles.title: USD/JPY: Intervention and weak core CPI cap Yen support – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Volkmar Baur reports that Japanese authorities appear to be intervening around USD/JPY 157 after the pair briefly hit 160.72. Tokyo inflation data show headline gains driven solely by energy, while core inflation has fallen to a one‐year low.

articles.title: Central bank policy errors may drive gold to $5,500/oz by Q1 2027: expert
articles.expand: Gold prices are likely to be much higher by the first quarter of 2027 despite struggling below the $4,600 per ounce currently. Short-term volatility and shifting macroeconomic risks raise the likelihood that central banks could make policy mistakes a backdrop that ultimately supports gold, Nitesh Shah, head of commodities and macroeconomic research...

articles.title: 'Poorer' Australians slugged for Middle East war: Bullock
articles.expand: Australians are poorer because of the ripple effect on prices from the conflict in the Middle East, RBA governor Michele Bullock has warned.

articles.title: RBA Strikes Again: Cash Rate Climbs to 4.35% as Inflation Stubbornly Rebounds
articles.expand: Declaring a 25-basis-point increase to the cash rate target to 4.35% as of May 5, 2026, the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) has stepped up its fight against ongoing price pressures. This action comes after a worrisome...

articles.title: NZ sharemarket rattled by Gentrack, down 0.4%
articles.expand: Gentrack rattled the New Zealand market when its share price was slashed by a third to a near-seven-year low following an earnings downgrade. Following a weak day on Wall Street, the S&P/NZX 50 Index fell at the open and traded steadily thereafter, closing at 13,035.7, down 61.98 points or 0.47%.

articles.title: Asian shares slip and oil pares gains on Iran war uncertainties
articles.expand: Asian stocks are trading lower, tracking losses on Wall Street after U.S. stocks fell from record highs. Oil prices fell back on Tuesday after jumping the day before as tensions between the U.S. and...

articles.title: Iran war leaves Qantas suppliers ‘nervous’ amid high jet fuel prices
articles.expand: The dislocations in the fuel market underscore the challenge for the aviation sector, as sharply higher prices and changes to air routes put pressure on businesses.

articles.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD attracts some sellers for the third straight day
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair trades with a negative bias for the third straight day on Tuesday, though it lacks follow-through selling and holds above the 1.3500 psychological mark during the Asian session.

articles.expand: With the RBNZ cautiously shifting to a more hawkish stance, a major miss in employment data could put them back in cautious mode. Here’s what I’m watching on AUD/NZD and NZD/JPY in this case.

articles.title: ECB forecasters slash growth and raise inflation outlook as energy shock bites
articles.expand: ECB professional forecasters have raised eurozone inflation expectations to 2.7% for 2026 while cutting growth forecasts to 1.0%, with energy costs from the Middle East war cited as the primary driver. Summary: The ECB's Survey of Professional Forecasters for Q2 2026 showed headline inflation expectations revised sharply upward to 2.7% for 2026, up...

articles.title: IMF says worst-case global economy warning is now the working assumption - oil shock hit
articles.expand: IMF chief Georgieva has abandoned the fund's mild slowdown forecast, warning that if the Middle East war continues into 2027 with oil at $125 a barrel, a "much worse" global economic outcome must be expected. Summary: The IMF's previous baseline forecast of a minor global growth slowdown and modest price increases from the Middle East conflict has ...

articles.title: War fears tarnish Metals – Silver (XAG/USD) breaks $75 & Gold (XAU/USD) tests $4,500
articles.expand: XAU/USD, XAG/USD Outlook: Precious metals decline amid escalating Middle East tensions and surging inflation worries. As gold and silver break lower, traders continue to question their role as safe havens in a tense environment. The action gets more bearish in this new week. Explore an intraday technical analysis of XAU/USD and XAG/USD.

Bearish EUR/USD isn't just a trade on Europe — it's a broad bet on dollar strength....

articles.title: RBNZ board member Gai says Hormuz shock does not warrant reflexive rate hikes
articles.expand: RBNZ board member Professor Prasanna Gai says the Hormuz supply shock does not imply reflexive tightening, but has raised the neutral rate. Pre-emptive hikes are only warranted when synchronisation is high. Summary: RBNZ board member Gai said the model of the Strait of Hormuz shock does not imply a reflexive tightening bias, per remarks attributed ...

articles.title: Weekly Forex Forecast – USD/JPY, S&P 500 Index, NASDAQ 100 Index, Brent Crude Oil, Gasoline
articles.expand: As the prospects of a peace deal between the USA and Iran fade on the reluctance of both sides to adjust red lines, energies rise, but US stocks are not deterred from hitting new highs, while the Forex market finally sees a Bank of Japan intervention to prop up the Yen.
